WV.url_status: working - page loads but report downloads are in .zip format
WV.launch_notes: First legal wager placed at Hollywood Casino at Charles Town Races. Legalized March 2018 via SB 415.
WV.reporting_notes: WV Lottery releases weekly figures on handle, GGR, and tax revenue. The weekly summary is published as a zipped file.
file_formats: ZIP (containing unknown inner format, likely CSV or Excel)
WV.archive_depth: Since August 2018 (launch). Weekly data available historically.
WV.requires_js_notes: Page is standard HTML with download links. ZIP file download is direct.
WV.operator_breakdown_notes: The weekly summary appears to be aggregate statewide data (not broken down by operator). Operator licensing info is in a separate PDF.
operators: DraftKings (partnered with Hollywood Casino at Charles Town Races)
FanDuel (partnered with The Greenbrier Casino Club)
BetMGM (partnered with The Greenbrier Casino Club)
Caesars Sportsbook (partnered with Mountaineer Casino Racetrack & Resort)
BetRivers (online-only partnership with Mountaineer Casino Racetrack)
Fanatics Sportsbook
theScore Bet (formerly ESPN BET, rebranded Dec 2025)
Betly (retail at Wheeling Island and Mardi Gras casinos)
Golden Nugget Online Sportsbook
retail_locations: Hollywood Casino at Charles Town Races
WV.sport_breakdown_notes: No sport-level breakdown in official weekly reports.
WV.retail_online_split_notes: The weekly summary does not appear to split retail vs online. Both casino-based retail and online sportsbooks operate in WV.

data_fields.name: Total Gross Sports Wagering Receipts
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered (handle) for the reporting period

data_fields.name: Adjusted Gross Sports Wagering Receipts
data_fields.description: Gross receipts minus winnings paid to wagerers

data_fields.name: Tax Amount
data_fields.description: 10% privilege tax on adjusted gross sports wagering receipts

WV.data_fields_notes: Per WV Code 29-22D, the weekly return filed by operators must include: (A) total gross and adjusted gross sports wagering receipts, (B) tax amount owed, (C) any additional information for tax computation. The publicly downloadable weekly summary is a condensed version.
WV.ggr_definition: Adjusted Gross Sports Wagering Receipts = Gross sports wagering receipts minus winnings paid to wagerers
WV.ggr_formula: AGR = Gross Receipts - Winnings Paid to Wagerers
WV.promo_deduction: No explicit promotional deduction mentioned in WV Code 29-22D. The definition of 'adjusted gross sports wagering receipts' only allows deduction of winnings paid. Promotional credits do not appear to be deductible from the tax base.
WV.tax_basis: Adjusted Gross Sports Wagering Receipts (gross receipts minus player winnings)
WV.tax_notes: First $15M of tax revenue goes to state lottery fund; excess goes to state pensions. A 2026 bill (HB 4398) proposed increasing to 25% but has not been enacted.
WV.scraping_approach: Download the ZIP file from the business portal, extract contents, parse the inner file (likely CSV/Excel). URL pattern for the weekly summary may need to be discovered by examining the download link. The page uses standard HTML links.
WV.scraping_time_estimate: 2-3 days to build scraper, depending on ZIP contents format
sample_files_downloaded: research/samples/WV/sports_wagering_license_list.pdf (HTML returned instead of PDF - auth required)
WV.notes: WV has both casino-based retail and online sportsbooks. The weekly reporting cadence is unique among states (most report monthly). The ZIP file format for reports is unusual. The business portal at business.wvlottery.com may require additional investigation to find the exact download URL pattern for weekly summaries. A separate Sports Wagering License List and Registrant List are available as PDFs.
WV.log: Fetched business.wvlottery.com - found Sports Wagering Weekly Summary as ZIP download. Searched for operator list, tax rates, GGR formula. WV Code 29-22D confirmed AGR definition and 10% tax. License list PDF download returned HTML (may need auth). No sport breakdown or operator breakdown in public reports.

ME.regulatory_body: Maine Gambling Control Unit (Department of Public Safety)
ME.launch_notes: Online sports wagering launched November 3, 2023. Governor Janet Mills signed legalization bill in May 2022. Retail (Oddfellahs by Caesars/First Tracks) launched September 2024.
ME.reporting_notes: Monthly reports published as individual PDFs per operator. Each operator has a separate PDF for each reporting period. Reports are organized by calendar year (Jan-Dec) with YTD totals.
ME.archive_depth: Since late 2023 (Passamaquoddy and Penobscot/Maliseet/Micmac from 2023; First Tracks from 2024; Oxford Sportsbook from late 2025)
ME.requires_js_notes: Standard maine.gov page with direct PDF download links
ME.operator_breakdown_notes: Each operator has its own separate PDF report. Reports are published individually by tribe/operator. However, within each PDF the data is aggregate for that operator (not broken down further).
operators: First Tracks Investments, LLC (Oddfellahs - retail, powered by Caesars Sportsbook)
Passamaquoddy Tribe (DraftKings Sportsbook - online)
Penobscot, Maliseet & Micmac Tribes (Caesars Sportsbook - online)
Oxford Sportsbook (Oxford Casino - new entrant as of late 2025)
ME.sport_breakdown_notes: No sport-level breakdown in official revenue reports.
ME.retail_online_split_notes: Reports are per-operator. First Tracks is retail, Passamaquoddy and Penobscot/Maliseet/Micmac are online, so retail vs online can be inferred from operator identity, but there is no explicit split within reports.

data_fields.name: Gross Event Wagering Receipts (Wagers)
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered (handle)

data_fields.name: Voided and Cancelled Wagers
data_fields.description: Wagers that were voided or cancelled (adjustment)

data_fields.name: Winnings Paid to Players (Payouts)
data_fields.description: Total amount paid out to winning bettors

data_fields.name: Federal Excise Tax - 0.25%
data_fields.description: Federal excise tax at 0.25% of handle

data_fields.name: Adjusted Gross Receipts
data_fields.description: Formula: (1) -/+ (2) - (3) - (4) - (5). Net revenue after all deductions

data_fields.name: Total Tax Revenue Due the State @10%
data_fields.description: 10% of Adjusted Gross Receipts

data_fields.name: General Fund
data_fields.description: Portion of tax allocated to General Fund (~65%)

data_fields.name: Administrative Expenses Gambling Control Unit
data_fields.description: 1% of AGR for GCU operations

data_fields.name: Gambling Addiction Prevention and Treatment
data_fields.description: 1% of AGR for addiction prevention

data_fields.name: Harness Racing Commission
data_fields.description: ~0.55% of AGR for harness racing

data_fields.name: Sire Stakes
data_fields.description: ~0.55% of AGR for sire stakes program

data_fields.name: Agricultural Fairs
data_fields.description: ~0.4% of AGR for agricultural fairs

ME.ggr_definition: Adjusted Gross Receipts (AGR) = Gross Event Wagering Receipts - Voided/Cancelled Wagers - Winnings Paid to Players - Federal Excise Tax (0.25%)
ME.ggr_formula: AGR = GrossWagers +/- Adjustments - Voids - Payouts - FederalExciseTax(0.25%)
ME.promo_deduction: The AGR formula explicitly shows deductions for voided/cancelled wagers and federal excise tax. Promotional credits may be included implicitly via adjustments line, but there is no explicit 'promotional deduction' line item in the published reports.
ME.scraping_approach: PDFs are directly downloadable from maine.gov. Each operator has individual PDFs by year. URL pattern: /dps/sites/maine.gov.dps/files/inline-files/website_{OperatorName}%20WE%20{MM}-{DD}-{YYYY}.pdf. Parse PDF tables to extract monthly rows. Reports are simple single-page PDFs with standard tabular format.
ME.notes: Maine's tribal-based licensing model is unique. DraftKings operates through the Passamaquoddy Tribe, Caesars operates through the Penobscot/Maliseet/Micmac Tribes. Oxford Sportsbook (Oxford Casino) joined in late 2025. Reports are per-operator PDFs with detailed AGR formula showing each deduction step including federal excise tax. Very clean, structured data suitable for automated parsing.
ME.log: Fetched maine.gov sports wagering revenue page - found 4 operators with individual PDF reports. Downloaded Passamaquoddy Feb 2026 PDF - confirmed single-page layout with monthly columns, clear AGR formula. Searched for tax rates (10%), promo deductions, GGR formula. Very structured data.

NH.launch_notes: DraftKings launched as sole operator in December 2019 under exclusive contract with NH Lottery. Contract recently renewed/extended through 2028.
NH.reporting_frequency: monthly (published as fiscal year cumulative PDF)
NH.reporting_notes: Reports are published as a single cumulative fiscal year PDF that gets updated each month. FY runs July-June. Each PDF shows all months of the current fiscal year in a single table. Historical FY PDFs available back to FY2020.
NH.archive_depth: FY2020 through FY2026 (since December 2019 launch). Six fiscal years of data.
NH.requires_js_notes: PDF links hosted on Contentful CDN (assets.ctfassets.net). Direct download URLs.
NH.operator_breakdown_notes: DraftKings is the sole operator, so all data represents DraftKings activity. No multi-operator breakdown needed.
operators: DraftKings (exclusive sole operator under state contract)
NH.operator_notes: DraftKings has exclusive rights. If 1-2 additional licenses were granted, the revenue share would drop from 51% to 21%. Contract renewed in Feb 2026.
NH.sport_breakdown_notes: No sport-level breakdown in published reports.
NH.retail_online_split_notes: Report clearly separates 'Mobile Sports Betting', 'Retail Sports Betting', and 'Combined' in three side-by-side tables.

data_fields.name: Handle
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered for the month

data_fields.name: GGR
data_fields.description: Gross Gaming Revenue - net revenue after payouts

data_fields.name: State Rev Share
data_fields.description: State's share of revenue (51% of GGR for mobile, ~45% for retail)

NH.data_fields_notes: Report shows three sections: Mobile Sports Betting, Retail Sports Betting, and Combined. Each section has Handle, GGR, and State Rev Share columns. Monthly rows plus fiscal year total. Report header says 'Unaudited'. FY26 shows Jul-25 through Feb-26 data.
NH.ggr_definition: GGR (Gross Gaming Revenue) = Handle minus payouts to winning bettors. This is the operator's net win.
NH.promo_deduction: Not explicitly shown in published reports. The GGR figure appears to already account for any promotional adjustments. No separate promo deduction line item.
NH.online_tax_rate: 51% revenue share (not traditional tax - state takes 51% of GGR)
NH.tax_basis: Gross Gaming Revenue (GGR) - revenue sharing model, not traditional tax
NH.tax_notes: NH uses a revenue sharing model rather than a traditional tax. DraftKings pays 51% of mobile GGR and approximately 45% of retail GGR to the state. Revenue primarily directed to public education. Lifetime revenue to schools exceeds $172M. If additional operators were added, the rate would drop to 21%.
NH.scraping_approach: Download single PDF per fiscal year from Contentful CDN. Parse the simple table structure - three side-by-side tables with monthly rows. URL pattern: assets.ctfassets.net/{space_id}/{entry_id}/{hash}/Sports_Betting_Summary_FY{XX}.pdf. Could also just download all 6 FY PDFs and extract complete history.
NH.notes: NH has the simplest reporting structure of the Tier 4 states due to having a single operator (DraftKings). The revenue share model (51%) is among the highest effective tax rates in the US. Reports are very clean single-page PDFs with clear mobile/retail/combined breakdown. DraftKings handles both online and retail locations. Contract recently extended through 2028.
NH.log: Fetched nhlottery.com financial reports page - found FY20-FY26 PDFs. Downloaded FY26 PDF - confirmed simple 3-column layout (Mobile/Retail/Combined) with Handle, GGR, State Rev Share. Searched for revenue share details (51% mobile). Very clean data format.

RI.launch_notes: Retail sports betting launched November 26, 2018 at Twin River Casino (now Bally's Lincoln). Online sports betting launched in 2019. One of the earliest states to legalize. Legislation signed June 22, 2018.
RI.reporting_frequency: monthly (published as fiscal year cumulative PDF)
RI.reporting_notes: Reports are published as cumulative fiscal year PDFs, updated monthly. FY runs July-June. Each PDF shows all months to date. Historical data back to FY2019.
RI.archive_depth: FY2019 through FY2026. Seven fiscal years of data since November 2018 launch.
RI.requires_js_notes: Direct PDF download links on rilot.com. URLs follow pattern: /content/dam/interactive/ilottery/pdfs/financial/{filename}.pdf
RI.operator_breakdown_notes: No operator-level breakdown since IGT/Bally's operates as sole provider. However, the report breaks data by FACILITY (Twin River, Tiverton Casino, Online/Mobile), which is functionally a venue breakdown.
operators: Sportsbook Rhode Island (operated by IGT in partnership with Bally's Corporation, managed by Rhode Island Lottery)
RI.operator_notes: Monopoly model. IGT provides technology, Bally's hosts the physical facilities. Revenue split: State 51%, IGT 32%, Bally's host facilities 17%. A 2025 bill (SB 748) to open the market to 3-5 competing operators stalled in the House. DraftKings may have been recently added as online operator.
RI.sport_breakdown_notes: No sport-level breakdown in published reports.
RI.retail_online_split_notes: Report breaks down by three venues: Twin River (retail), Tiverton Casino (retail), and Online (Mobile). Combined totals also shown.

data_fields.name: Write (Accrual)
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered (handle) on accrual basis

data_fields.name: Payout (Accrual)
data_fields.description: Total payouts to winning bettors on accrual basis

data_fields.name: Book Revenue*
data_fields.description: Net revenue = Write minus Payout. *Before any commissions, operating, or allocable expenses

RI.data_fields_notes: Report has four sections side-by-side: Twin River, Tiverton Casino, Online (Mobile), and Combined. Each shows Write, Payout, and Book Revenue columns. Monthly rows with fiscal year totals. Header says 'Unaudited and Unadjusted'. Note: Book Revenue is before commissions/expenses.
RI.ggr_definition: Book Revenue = Write (total wagers) minus Payout (total payouts). This is gross revenue before any commissions, operating, or allocable expenses.
RI.promo_deduction: No promotional deduction line item shown in published reports. Book Revenue is explicitly noted as 'before any commissions, operating, or allocable expenses.' Promo treatment unclear from published data.
RI.online_tax_rate: 51% revenue share to state (not traditional tax)
RI.retail_tax_rate: 51% revenue share to state (same for retail)
RI.tax_notes: Revenue sharing model: State receives 51%, IGT receives 32%, Bally's host facilities receive 17%. Each facility town (Lincoln and Tiverton) receives $200K annual flat fee. Among highest effective rates in the nation alongside NH and NY.
RI.scraping_approach: Download PDF per fiscal year from rilot.com. Parse simple table with four facility sections. URL pattern: /content/dam/interactive/ilottery/pdfs/financial/{MonthAbbrev}_SportsbookWebsiteData.pdf for recent reports or SportsBookSummaryFY{YYYY}.pdf for historical. Single-page PDFs with clean tabular layout.
RI.notes: RI was one of the earliest states to launch sports betting (Nov 2018). The monopoly model with IGT/Bally's results in very simple reporting - just three venues. RI uses unique terminology: 'Write' instead of 'Handle', 'Book Revenue' instead of GGR. The 51/32/17 revenue split is distinctive. Data is clean and structured. There is legislative pressure to open the market to multiple operators (SB 748 in 2025).
RI.log: Fetched rilot.com financials page - found FY2019-FY2026 PDFs. Downloaded Jan FY26 PDF - confirmed facility breakdown (Twin River, Tiverton, Online, Combined) with Write/Payout/Book Revenue columns. Searched for revenue sharing structure (51/32/17 split). Very clean single-page PDF format.

WY.url_status: partially working - page loads but requires JavaScript to render report links
WY.launch_notes: Online sports betting launched September 2021. Legalized April 2021. Online/mobile only - no retail sportsbooks permitted in Wyoming.
WY.reporting_notes: Wyoming Gaming Commission publishes Combined Wagering Activity Reports monthly. These cover all gaming types (HHR, simulcasting, sports wagering, etc.) in one report. Archive reports also available at gaming.wyo.gov/historical-revenue-reports/osw.
WY.file_format_notes: Reports appear to be PDF format. The Combined Wagering Activity Reports include sports wagering as one section among several gaming types.
WY.archive_depth: September 2021 through present. Monthly reports since launch. Historical archive at gaming.wyo.gov/historical-revenue-reports/osw.
WY.requires_js_notes: The Wyoming Gaming Commission website uses JavaScript framework to render page content. Report links may not be accessible via simple HTTP fetch. Requires browser-based scraping or API discovery.
WY.operator_breakdown_notes: Combined Wagering Activity Reports include operator-level breakdowns in the Online Sports Wagering section, showing individual sportsbook performance.
operators: DraftKings
FanDuel
BetMGM
Caesars Sportsbook
Fanatics Sportsbook
WY.operator_notes: Originally 6 operators including ESPN BET, which rebranded to theScore Bet in late 2025. Current count may be 5-6 depending on active licenses. All are online-only.
WY.sport_breakdown_notes: No sport-level breakdown in published reports.
WY.retail_online_split_notes: Wyoming is online-only. No retail sportsbooks exist. All revenue is from mobile/online platforms.

data_fields.name: Handle
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered

data_fields.name: Gross Gaming Revenue (GGR)
data_fields.description: Revenue after payouts - total wagers minus winnings paid

data_fields.name: Tax Revenue
data_fields.description: 10% tax on GGR

data_fields.name: Hold %
data_fields.description: GGR as percentage of Handle (retention rate)

data_fields.name: Non-Cash Payouts / Promotional Spending
data_fields.description: Promotional play amounts (may be deductible)

WY.data_fields_notes: The Combined Wagering Activity Reports include sports wagering alongside HHR, simulcasting, and other gaming data. Sports wagering fields include handle, GGR, tax, and potentially promotional spending breakdowns by operator.
WY.ggr_definition: GGR = Total Handle (wagers) minus Total Payouts (winnings paid to bettors)
WY.promo_deduction: Wyoming appears to allow operators to deduct free wagers and promotional play from gross gaming revenue for tax purposes. Specific promotional deduction rules are in the Wyoming Gaming Commission's administrative rules.
WY.tax_basis: Gross Gaming Revenue (GGR), potentially after promotional deductions
WY.tax_notes: 10% tax on GGR. In June 2025, the Wyoming Legislature's Select Committee on Capital Financing and Investments proposed doubling the tax to 20%, but this has not been enacted. Wyoming generated approximately $1.5M in annual tax revenue from sports betting.
WY.scraping_approach: The Wyoming Gaming Commission website uses a JavaScript framework that prevents simple HTTP fetching of report links. Options: (1) Use Selenium/Playwright to render the page and extract download URLs, (2) Try the historical archive at gaming.wyo.gov/historical-revenue-reports/osw which may have more accessible links, (3) Contact the Commission for direct data access. Reports are PDFs that would need PDF parsing.
WY.scraping_time_estimate: 3-5 days due to JS rendering requirements
WY.notes: Wyoming is unique as an online-only market with no retail sportsbooks. Despite being a small market ($78.9M handle through Apr 2025), it has a structured regulatory framework. The Combined Wagering Activity Reports bundle sports wagering with other gaming types (HHR, racing, etc.), making extraction require filtering for the sports wagering section. The JS-heavy website and small market size make this a lower-priority scraping target. DraftKings dominates nearly half the market.
WY.log: Fetched gaming.wyo.gov pages - JS rendering prevented full content extraction. Searched for report format details, operator list, tax rates. Found 5 operators (DraftKings, FanDuel, BetMGM, Caesars, Fanatics). Confirmed 10% tax rate, online-only model. Could not download sample reports due to JS-rendered pages. Archive URL identified at /historical-revenue-reports/osw.

MO.launch_notes: Sports betting legalized via voter ballot measure in November 2024 (passed by just 2,691 votes out of ~3M cast). Launched December 1, 2025 with 8 online and 8 retail operators.
MO.reporting_notes: MGC publishes two report types monthly: (1) SW Monthly Financials - summary by retail/mobile with operator breakdown, (2) SW Revenue Detail Report - detailed breakdown by operator AND sport. Both available in PDF and Excel. Organized by fiscal year.
MO.archive_depth: Only 2 months available: December 2025 and January 2026 (launched Dec 1, 2025). Very new market.
MO.requires_js_notes: Standard HTML page with direct download links. URL pattern: mgc.dps.mo.gov/SportsWagering/sw_financials/FY26_SWFinReport/{MM}_{MonthName}/SW%20{ReportType}%20{MMYY}.{ext}
MO.operator_breakdown_notes: Reports break down data by individual operator for both retail and mobile. Summary report shows operator-level totals. Detail report shows operator + sport breakdown.
MO.sport_breakdown_notes: Detail reports provide sport-by-sport breakdown per operator. 'No wager shall be reported in more than one category. All parlay wagers shall be reported in the parlay category.' Note: Detail sport totals may not match Monthly Financials due to additional deductions (federal taxes, promotional play, promotional merchandise) in AGR calculation.
MO.retail_online_split_notes: Summary report has separate sections for RETAIL STATS and MOBILE STATS with subtotals. Detail report has separate retail and mobile sections. In first month, 99.23% of handle was mobile.

data_fields_summary_report.name: Number of Wagers
data_fields_summary_report.description: Total count of individual wagers placed

data_fields_summary_report.name: Total Deductions
data_fields_summary_report.description: Total deductions including payouts, promos, federal taxes

data_fields_summary_report.name: Total Taxable AGR
data_fields_summary_report.description: Adjusted Gross Revenue subject to state tax. Can be negative.

data_fields_summary_report.name: Sports Wagering Tax
data_fields_summary_report.description: 10% tax on positive Taxable AGR. $0 when AGR is negative.

data_fields_detail_report.name: Sport Category
data_fields_detail_report.description: Sport type (Baseball, Basketball, Boxing/MMA, Football, Hockey, Soccer, Tennis, Motor Racing, Parlay, Other)

data_fields_detail_report.name: Number of Wagers
data_fields_detail_report.description: Count of wagers for that sport

data_fields_detail_report.name: Total Wagers/Handle*
data_fields_detail_report.description: Dollar amount wagered on that sport

data_fields_detail_report.name: Total Payout*
data_fields_detail_report.description: Dollar amount paid out to winners for that sport

data_fields_detail_report.name: Total Wagers Minus Payouts
data_fields_detail_report.description: Handle minus payouts (gross win before deductions)

MO.data_fields_notes: The Summary (Monthly Financials) report uses 'Total Deductions' which includes payouts + promos + federal taxes. The Detail report shows only Handle and Payout by sport (no promo deductions at sport level). Detail totals may not match Summary AGR because promos/federal taxes are applied at operator level, not sport level.
MO.ggr_definition: Taxable AGR (Adjusted Gross Revenue) = Total Handle - Total Deductions (which includes payouts, promotional play, promotional merchandise, and federal taxes)
MO.ggr_formula: Taxable AGR = Handle - Payouts - Promotional Play - Promotional Merchandise - Federal Taxes
MO.promo_deduction: Missouri allows sportsbooks to deduct up to 25% of their total taxable revenue for free bets and promotional credits given to incentivize gambling. This constitutional provision (from the 2024 ballot measure) has resulted in heavily negative AGR statewide during launch months. In Dec 2025 first month: $543M handle but negative AGR of -$21.6M for mobile due to massive promotional spending. A 2026 bill has been proposed to address this.
MO.tax_basis: Taxable AGR (Adjusted Gross Revenue after all deductions including promos)
MO.tax_notes: 10% tax on Taxable AGR. When AGR is negative (due to promo deductions exceeding revenue), the operator pays $0 tax for that period. The generous promo deduction allowance has resulted in minimal state tax revenue despite massive handle. In first 2 months: $928M handle but only $659K in total tax revenue. Bills under consideration to cap or eliminate promo deductions.
MO.scraping_approach: Download PDF or Excel files directly from MGC website. Excel files (XLSX) are the most scraper-friendly. URL pattern: mgc.dps.mo.gov/SportsWagering/sw_financials/FY{YY}_SWFinReport/{MM}_{MonthName}/SW%20{ReportType}%20{MMYY}.xlsx. Two report types per month. Very structured tabular data.

sample_data_jan26.top_operator_by_handle: DraftKings ($144.4M) and FanDuel ($125.7M) combined for 71% of mobile handle

MO.notes: Missouri is the newest state in this tier, having just launched December 1, 2025. It has the most comprehensive reporting of all Tier 4 states - with operator breakdown, sport breakdown, retail/mobile split, and both summary and detail reports available in both PDF and Excel. The generous promotional deduction rules (up to 25% of revenue) have resulted in negative AGR and minimal tax revenue despite massive handle. The Excel format makes this the easiest state to scrape despite being the newest. MO reports are the gold standard for data richness in Tier 4.
MO.log: Fetched MGC sports wagering page - found 2 report types (Monthly Financials, Revenue Detail) in PDF+Excel. Downloaded all 4 files for Jan 2026. Confirmed structure from PDF: Summary has Retail/Mobile with operator-level breakdown; Detail has per-operator per-sport breakdown with 10 sport categories. Searched for promo deduction rules (25% cap), AGR formula, tax rate (10%). Excellent data quality and accessibility.
